page: Vultr voc-c-32c-64gb-1000s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 32 vCPUs and 32 physical cores running at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz, with a shared CPU allocation. The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled, consisting of a 1000 GB NVMe SSD.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates strong processing capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance score of 3132.38 places it in the top tier (top 10%), while the multi-core CPU performance score of 99610.25 is rated as strong (top 25%). These metrics reflect the capabilities of the AMD EPYC Milan architecture under compute-heavy workloads, despite the shared CPU allocation model.

The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio positions this server as a compute-focused option. The shared CPU allocation represents a potential tradeoff for workloads requiring sustained, isolated CPU performance, but it offers a cost-efficient alternative for high-density compute tasks that can tolerate shared resources. With its bundled 1000 GB NVMe SSD and high core count, this server is suited for multi-threaded processing, batch compilation, web serving, and database workloads that benefit from fast local storage and high parallel processing capacity.
description: Vultr voc-c-32c-64gb-1000s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server configured with an AMD EPYC Milan processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 32 vCPUs and 32 physical cores operating at 2.0 GHz under a shared CPU allocation model. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding 2.0 GB of memory per core, and includes a bundled 1000 GB NVMe SSD for high-speed local storage. In performance benchmarks, the instance achieves a top-tier single-core CPU score of 3132.38 and a strong multi-core CPU score of 99610.25. This hardware profile offers a cost-effective, compute-heavy resource allocation. It is well-suited for parallel processing, multi-threaded applications, and database workloads that require substantial CPU cores and fast local storage without requiring dedicated CPU threads.

tagline: A compute-focused virtual server featuring thirty-two AMD EPYC cores, sixty-four gigabytes of RAM, and fast local NVMe storage.
